conditions.evidence_text: On polymer-based RP columns, which do not contain silanol groups, the CAP for PO (at about 90% acetone) can be utilized, as PEG elutes under these conditions in SEC mode... Figure 4. Chromatograms of PPG 1000, water, and two poloxamers, as obtained on the Jordi column in 91.73 wt-% acetone – water.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical Adsorption Point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.notes: Used to analyze poloxamers (EO-PO-EO) where EO is at CAP and PO elutes in SEC mode. The text mentions '90%' generally but Figure 4 specifies '91.73 wt-%'.

conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
conditions.evidence_text: This can be achieved on a diol column in about 80 wt-% acetone... Figure 5. Chromatograms of poloxamers and PEGs, as obtained on the Diol column in 78.54 wt-% acetone - water.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical Adsorption Point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.notes: Normal phase column. EO is at CAP, PO elutes in SEC mode.

conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
conditions.evidence_text: This can be achieved on RP columns at the first critical point in acetone – water with about 30 wt-% acetone... Figure 6. Chromatograms of different diblock and triblock oligomers... as obtained on the Jordi column in 30 wt-% acetone – water.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical Adsorption Point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.notes: EO is at CAP, PO elutes in LAC mode. Used for short PO blocks.

conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
conditions.evidence_text: as can be seen in Figure 7, a CAP for EO exits on the Novapak column in 80 wt-% methanol... Figure 8. Chromatograms of PPG 1000 and different triblock copolymers, as obtained on the Novapak column in 80 wt-% acetone – water.
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical Adsorption Point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.notes: Text states '80 wt-% methanol' in the discussion of Figure 7, but Figure 8 caption says '80 wt-% acetone'. The text explicitly links the CAP for EO on Novapak to methanol-water in the paragraph preceding Figure 7. The Figure 8 caption likely contains a typo or refers to a different run not explicitly detailed as the CAP condition in the text body. Based on the explicit text 'CAP for EO exits on the Novapak column in 80 wt-% methanol', methanol is extracted. The Figure 8 caption mentions acetone, but the text body links the CAP finding to methanol.
